title: ABNF, Tokens, Whitespace, Line Endings, Limits, and Parser Architecture · Go Semantics Case: Abnf Derivation
summary: A parser splits strings freely, accepts bare line feeds and whitespace before fields, has no byte limits, and mixes syntax recognition with application side effects. Planned artifact: Add and verify the abnf derivation increment in strict bounded incremental parser architecture. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Translate RFC ABNF alternatives, repetition, terminals, lists, and prose constraints into explicit parser states and tests.
Trace origin, proxy, gateway, tunnel, cache, and user-agent transformations while retaining end-to-end and hop evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 3255 in a fuzzing harness varying every legal input segmentation.
